meta_title: Houses With Black Roofs: 210 Examples + Recommended Shingles (2026)
meta_description: Real houses with black roofs: AI-rendered examples across 6 architectural sub-styles, the top siding colors and home styles that pair with black, a curated list of the best black-roof shingles from every major brand, and a free visualizer that lets you swap your roof color in 4 seconds.

hub_hero.h1_html: Houses With Black Roofs: 210 Examples 2026
hub_hero.intro: Black is the most-searched roof color in America and the fastest-growing specification in the architectural-shingle category. Endlessly versatile, instantly modern, and the safest bet for resale. Works on every house style from coastal cottage to mountain lodge, and pairs especially well with white, cream, light-gray, sage, and navy siding. Search volume has tripled since 2018, driven by the modern-farmhouse design wave and a broader trend toward graphic, high-contrast residential palettes. Performance-wise, black asphalt and standing-seam metal both carry 25-to-50-year manufacturer warranties; the choice between materials is now purely aesthetic and budget-driven for most Florida builds.

materials.kicker: Material variations · how black is built
materials.title_html: Two ways to get a black roof.

cards.body: The everyday workhorse. Dimensional shadow line, 25-30 year warranty, 110-130 mph wind rating. Installed in 7 out of 10 American homes. $14k to $22k for a full Florida re-roof.
The modern farmhouse signature. Vertical seam lines, 50-year warranty, reflects heat. About 2x the cost of asphalt but lasts twice as long. The sharpest expression of "black roof".

items.label: GAF Camelot II Camelot Ii Charcoal
GAF Grand Sequoia Grand Sequoia Charcoal
GAF Timberline AS II Timberline As Ii Charcoal

columns.title: Pair black with a house color

items.label: Light Gray House With Black Roof
Sage Green House With Black Roof

faq_items.q: What color metal roof goes with a white house?
faq_items.a: Black is the most popular pairing, with charcoal a close second. The high contrast between a white siding and a near-black roof reads 'modern American classic' and works on every architectural style from cottage to colonial. Avoid stark cool whites under a black roof -- slightly warm whites like Benjamin Moore Simply White or Sherwin-Williams Alabaster pair best.

faq_items.q: Do black roofs make a house hotter?
faq_items.a: Modestly. A black roof adds about 5 to 10 degrees Fahrenheit to attic temperatures during peak Florida summer compared to a light-gray roof. With modern attic ventilation and a radiant barrier, the actual impact on monthly cooling costs is typically under 20 dollars. Most homeowners find the aesthetic worth the trade-off.

faq_items.q: Does a black roof affect home insurance in Florida?
faq_items.a: Not because of the color. Florida insurers care about wind rating, hail rating, and roof age. Every black-roof SKU we cover is 130 mph wind and Class A fire. For impact discounts, look for Class 3 or 4 (hail-rated) variants -- available across most of the recommended brands.

faq_items.q: What is the most popular black shingle?
faq_items.a: By install volume: GAF Timberline HDZ Charcoal. By design-magazine appearances: Owens Corning Duration Onyx Black. By Florida hurricane-zone preference: CertainTeed Landmark Pro Moire Black. All three are within a few dollars per square of each other at retail.

faq_items.q: How long does a black roof last?
faq_items.a: Asphalt architectural: 25 to 35 years with proper installation. Metal standing seam: 50 to 70 years. Color holds through most of that span thanks to ceramic-coated granules (asphalt) or factory-baked Kynar finish (metal).

faq_items.q: Will a black roof show algae or streaks?
faq_items.a: Algae streaks are more visible on light-colored roofs (where they appear as dark vertical streaks). On a black roof, the streaks blend in and are essentially invisible. Algae warranty terms still apply but it is rare to ever notice the streaks.
